parentReportJson.summary: Procedure gap is the clearest repeat pattern across this upload, with Calculation slip showing up as the next focus area.

topFindings.title: Procedure gap
topFindings.whatToDo: Rebuild the step order with slow, spoken practice before adding harder problems.
topFindings.rationale: The student setup shows the right ingredients but the step order breaks before the result.

topFindings.title: Calculation slip
topFindings.whatToDo: Keep the same method but add a deliberate final arithmetic check.
topFindings.rationale: The method is mostly correct, but the arithmetic execution slips near the finish.

topFindings.title: Strategy error
topFindings.whatToDo: Compare two solving strategies and choose the simpler one before starting.
topFindings.rationale: The student chooses a weaker strategy than the page seems to require.

evidenceGroups.description: The student knows the concept but loses the correct step order.

parentReportJson.doThisWeek: Start by stabilizing procedure gap before introducing harder mixed practice.
parentReportJson.notNow: Do not turn this report into direct homework answers or jump to harder packets before the repeated pattern settles.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Name the pattern behind Procedure gap.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Review two worked examples and explain why the method matters.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask your child to narrate the first step before writing anything.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: They can describe the setup in words before solving.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Slow down the procedure for Procedure gap.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Solve two short items while speaking each step out loud.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Pause after each line and ask what changed from the line above.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: The order of steps stays consistent without guessing.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Catch slips linked to Calculation slip.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Do one warm-up and two error-correction items from the same skill family.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask which symbol, sign, or number is easiest to lose when rushing.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: Your child can self-correct at least one slip before you point to it.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Mix old and new without increasing pressure.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Use two mixed questions and one short reflection after each answer.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask which problem felt more stable today and why.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: They can compare one strong response and one wobbly response.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Rehearse Procedure gap in a new context.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Try one worksheet-style item and one verbal explanation prompt.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask how they know they picked the right starting strategy.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: They choose the method with less prompting than earlier in the week.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Practice checking instead of racing to finish.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Complete one timed-light practice set with a final check pass.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask what they check first when they think they are done.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: They perform a deliberate final check on signs, units, or notation.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Wrap the week with confidence and one small stretch.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Revisit one earlier mistake and one mixed challenge item.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask what feels easier now and what still needs one more week.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: They can explain one improvement and one next focus area.

sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Pause after each line and ask what changed from the line above.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: The order of steps stays consistent without guessing.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Catch slips linked to error checking.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Do one warm-up and two error-correction items from the same skill family.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask which symbol, sign, or number is easiest to lose when rushing.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: Your child can self-correct at least one slip before you point to it.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Mix old and new without increasing pressure.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Use two mixed questions and one short reflection after each answer.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask which problem felt more stable today and why.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: They can compare one strong response and one wobbly response.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Rehearse core math procedure in a new context.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Try one worksheet-style item and one verbal explanation prompt.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask how they know they picked the right starting strategy.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: They choose the method with less prompting than earlier in the week.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Practice checking instead of racing to finish.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Complete one timed-light practice set with a final check pass.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask what they check first when they think they are done.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: They perform a deliberate final check on signs, units, or notation.

sevenDayPlan.goal: Wrap the week with confidence and one small stretch.
sevenDayPlan.practice: Revisit one earlier mistake and one mixed challenge item.
sevenDayPlan.parentPrompt: Ask what feels easier now and what still needs one more week.
sevenDayPlan.successSignal: They can explain one improvement and one next focus area.

sourceMetaJson.diagnosticGoal: Find the main repeated math errors in this packet.
sourceMetaJson.recentTrend: Wrong answers cluster around arithmetic and operation choice.

laterNodesJson.title: Stabilize concept understanding
laterNodesJson.copy: Re-upload clearer pages or use a text-based PDF before acting on this draft diagnosis.

laterNodesJson.title: Build solution process
laterNodesJson.copy: Learn the standard process without adding pressure.

laterNodesJson.title: Transfer to new problem types
laterNodesJson.copy: Apply the same structure in changed surfaces.

laterNodesJson.title: Strengthen speed and accuracy
laterNodesJson.copy: Improve fluency only after the structure holds.

shortestPath.whyFirst: Readable evidence has to come first, otherwise the diagnosis becomes guesswork.
shortestPath.whatThisSolves: It lets the next diagnosis run use real worksheet evidence instead of placeholders.
shortestPath.whatWaits: All teaching decisions should wait until the upload can be read reliably.

outputGates.title: Explain
outputGates.body: Explain in your own words why current understanding is the current focus.
outputGates.whatThisVerifies: This is the highest-leverage place to rebuild before adding more difficulty.
outputGates.howToCheck: Ask the child to name the whole, the parts, and the reason the first step makes sense before solving.

outputGates.title: Rebuild
outputGates.body: Rebuild a mistaken example and point out where current understanding starts to drift.
outputGates.whatThisVerifies: Re-upload clearer pages or use a text-based PDF before acting on this draft diagnosis.
outputGates.howToCheck: Show one mistaken solution, then ask the child to mark the first broken step and rebuild from there.

outputGates.title: Transfer
outputGates.body: Try one similar problem with a changed surface while keeping current understanding stable.
outputGates.whatThisVerifies: Whether current understanding survives when the wording changes.
outputGates.howToCheck: Use this only after the child can already explain and rebuild without much prompting.

outputGates.title: Spot the mistake
outputGates.body: Find the first step where the work breaks and explain why it matters.
outputGates.whatThisVerifies: Whether the child can identify the first meaningful break instead of only noticing the final wrong answer.
outputGates.howToCheck: Keep the reflection short. The goal is to identify the break clearly, not to create a long correction session.

compareSnapshot.stillUneven: The process holds in guided tasks but weakens under changed wording.

errorLabels.description: The student knows the concept but loses the correct step order.
The setup is mostly correct, but arithmetic execution slips.
The student uses inconsistent signs, symbols, or formatting.
The student chooses an inefficient or mismatched solving strategy.

itemErrors.rationale: The student setup shows the right ingredients but the step order breaks before the result.
